message.content: can you put claude code on a macbook pro

content.thinking: The user is asking about installing Claude Code on a MacBook Pro. This is a question about Claude Code itself - how to install/use it. I should use the Task tool with subagent_type='claude-code-guide' to get accurate information from the official documentation.

input.prompt: How do I install Claude Code on a Mac/MacBook? Provide the installation steps and any requirements.

content.text:
Based on the official Claude Code documentation, here's how to install Claude Code on a Mac/MacBook:

## System Requirements

Before installing, ensure your Mac meets these requirements:

- macOS 13.0 or later
- 4 GB+ RAM
- Internet connection
- A supported shell (Bash or Zsh, which are default on macOS)

## Installation Steps

You have two options for installing Claude Code on Mac:

### Option 1: Native Install (Recommended)

Open your terminal and run:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
```

This is the recommended approach because:
- No dependencies required
- Automatically updates in the background
- Faster setup

### Option 2: Homebrew

If you prefer Homebrew:

```bash
brew install --cask claude-code
```

Note: Homebrew installations do not auto-update. You'll need to manually run `brew upgrade claude-code` periodically to get the latest features and security fixes.

## Post-Installation

After installation completes:

1. **Verify the installation** by checking the version:
   ```bash
   claude --version
   ```

2. **Log in to your account**:
   ```bash
   cd /path/to/your/project
   claude
   ```
   
   When you run `claude` for the first time, your browser will open and prompt you to log in. You can use any of these account types:
   - Claude Pro, Max, Teams, or Enterprise subscription
   - Claude Console account (with pre-paid API credits)
   - Third-party providers (Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, or Microsoft Foundry)

3. **Start your first session**:
   ```bash
   claude
   ```
   You'll see the welcome screen. Type `/help` to see available commands.

## Alternative: Claude Code Desktop App

If you prefer a graphical interface, you can download the [Claude Code Desktop app for macOS](https://claude.ai/api/desktop/darwin/universal/dmg/latest/redirect?utm_source=claude_code&utm_medium=docs), which provides the same functionality without needing the terminal.

## Troubleshooting

If you encounter any issues, run:

```bash
claude doctor
```

This provides a detailed check of your installation and configuration.

For more help, see the [troubleshooting guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/troubleshooting.md).

Sources:
- [Claude Code Setup Documentation](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/setup.md)
- [Claude Code Quickstart Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/quickstart.md)

content.thinking: The agent provided comprehensive information about installing Claude Code on a Mac. Let me summarize this for the user.

content.text:
Yes, Claude Code works on MacBook Pro. Here's how to install it:

## Requirements
- macOS 13.0 or later
- 4 GB+ RAM
- Internet connection

## Installation

**Option 1: Native Install (Recommended)**
```bash
curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash
```

**Option 2: Homebrew**
```bash
brew install --cask claude-code
```

## After Installation

1. Verify it installed:
   ```bash
   claude --version
   ```

2. Start Claude Code in your project directory:
   ```bash
   cd /path/to/your/project
   claude
   ```

3. Your browser will open to log in with your Claude account (Pro, Max, Teams, Enterprise, or API credits)

There's also a **Desktop app** if you prefer a GUI over the terminal: download from [claude.ai](https://claude.ai/api/desktop/darwin/universal/dmg/latest/redirect).

If you run into issues, `claude doctor` will diagnose your setup.
